# Rate Hero

> For a concise summary, see https://goratehero.com/llms.txt

> Rate Hero is a licensed mortgage company (NMLS #2822806) specializing in DSCR loans, Non-QM mortgages, bank statement loans, and hard money exit refinances for real estate investors and self-employed borrowers. Available in 30+ states.

## About

Rate Hero, Inc. is a non-QM mortgage lender focused on real estate investors and self-employed borrowers. The company offers DSCR loans, bank statement loans, P&L loans, asset utilization loans, WVOE loans, investment property HELOCs, and hard money exit refinances. No W-2 or tax returns required for most programs. Typical close time is 21 days. Founder: Sean Davoodian (NMLS #1252107).

- Website: https://goratehero.com
- Company NMLS: 2822806
- Loan officer: Sean Davoodian, NMLS #1252107

## Loan Programs

- DSCR loans — Investment property mortgages qualified on the property's rental income via debt service coverage ratio rather than the borrower's personal income.
- No-ratio DSCR loans — DSCR loans that do not require a minimum debt service coverage ratio, qualifying the property without rent-to-payment math.
- Bank statement loans (12-month) — Self-employed mortgages that use 12 months of personal or business bank deposits to calculate qualifying income.
- Bank statement loans (24-month) — Self-employed mortgages that use 24 months of personal or business bank deposits to calculate qualifying income.
- Profit and loss (P&L) loans — Self-employed mortgages qualified using a CPA-prepared profit and loss statement instead of tax returns.
- Asset utilization / asset depletion loans — Mortgages qualified by dividing eligible liquid assets over a fixed term to derive monthly qualifying income.
- WVOE (written verification of employment) loans — Mortgages qualified using a signed verification of employment form in lieu of pay stubs or tax returns.
- Investment property HELOC — Second-lien revolving line of credit secured by a non-owner-occupied rental property.
- Hard money exit refinance — Long-term refinance designed to pay off a short-term hard money or bridge loan after stabilization.
- DSCR cash-out refinance — DSCR refinance that pulls equity out of a rental property without requiring personal income documentation.
- Foreign national investor loans — Mortgages for non-US-citizen, non-resident borrowers purchasing investment property in the United States.
- Interest-only DSCR loans — DSCR mortgages with an interest-only period that lowers monthly payment and improves the qualifying DSCR.
- DSCR loans for short-term rentals / Airbnb — DSCR loans qualified using short-term rental projections (AirDNA / market rent comparables) instead of long-term lease income.
- Bridge to DSCR refinance — Sequenced financing that uses a short-term bridge loan to acquire or rehab a property and a DSCR loan to take it out long-term.
- Blanket DSCR loans / portfolio loans — Single mortgages that cross-collateralize multiple investment properties under one note.

## Glossary Terms

- DSCR (debt service coverage ratio) — Ratio of gross rental income to total mortgage payment (PITIA) used to underwrite investment property loans.
- No-ratio DSCR — DSCR program that waives the minimum coverage threshold and qualifies the property without rent-to-payment math.
- Non-QM — Non-Qualified Mortgage; loans outside the CFPB Qualified Mortgage rule, typically using alternative income documentation.
- Debt yield — Net operating income divided by loan amount, used to size commercial and large-balance investment loans.
- Cash-on-cash return — Annual pre-tax cash flow divided by total cash invested in a rental property.
- 1007 rent schedule — Fannie Mae appraisal addendum that estimates market rent for a single-family investment property.
- Stabilized rental property — A property that is rented (or rentable) at market terms and producing predictable cash flow.
- Bridge loan — Short-term financing used to acquire, rehab, or carry a property until permanent financing is in place.
- Hard money loan — Short-term, asset-based loan typically funded by private capital with higher rates and faster close times.
- Seasoning requirements — Minimum ownership or title-seasoning period before a borrower can refinance or pull cash out.
- Prepayment penalty — Fee charged for paying off a loan before a contractually defined date; common on DSCR loans.
- Interest-only mortgage — Mortgage with an initial period during which only interest is paid, with no principal amortization.
- LLC vesting — Holding title to investment property in the name of a limited liability company.
- Entity borrower — A non-individual borrower such as an LLC, corporation, or limited partnership taking title to the property.
- Asset depletion — Method that converts liquid assets into qualifying monthly income by dividing them over a set term.
- Bank statement income calculation — Self-employed income derived from analyzing 12 or 24 months of business or personal bank deposits.
